revision.text:
[[ja:法吉神社]] {{Expand Japanese|法吉神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hokki Shrine | native_name = 法吉神社 | image = Hokkijinjahonden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hokki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.488056|133.045139}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Hokki Shrine'''|法吉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11395853|ltq=Q11395853|qid=Q11395853|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izumo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 60 ** P155: {{ill|Q135040928|ltq=Q135040928|qid=Q135040928|1=Nisanokashinowino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ikumano Shrine|lt=Ikumano Shrine|qid=Q135040930|1=Ikumano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q207321|ltq=Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} * {{ill|Q582846|ltq=Q582846|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}} * {{ill|Shimane district|lt=Shimane district|ja|島根郡|zh|島根郡|qid=Q11052337|1=Shimane district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.488056|133.045139}} == image (P18) == * Hokkijinjahonden.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182832 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ほっきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9280005000959 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県松江市法吉町583', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県松江市法吉町582', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:法吉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11555072}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hokki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Hokkijinjahonden.JPG|270px|Hokki Shrine main sanctuary]]
Main sanctuary |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}} Hokki-chō 583 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|17|N|133|2|42.5|E|}} |祭神 = Umukaihihime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|qid=Q387874|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |別名 = Ōmori Daimyōjin |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Hokki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hokki Shrine''' (ほっきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Hokki-chō, {{ill|lt=Matsue|Q207321|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === ;Main deity: {{ill|lt=Kisagaihime and Umugihime|Q11296639|qid=Q11296639|lt=Umukaihihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kisagaihime and Umugihime}} (うむかひひめのみこと) ;Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} ;Combined hall (Tsutsumi Shrine): {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}} In the Hokki-go section of {{ill|lt=Shimane District|Q11052337|qid=Q11052337|1=Shimane district}} in the ''{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}'', there is a tale that Umukaihihime-no-Mikoto transformed into the Hokki bird (hohokidori, i.e. the {{ill|lt=Japanese bush warbler|Q27075456|qid=Q27075456|1=Japanese bush warbler}}), flew to this district, and enshrined herself there, and that is why the place came to be called Hokki. Umukaihihime-no-Mikoto is considered the same deity as Umugi-hime (clam-shell princess) who appears in {{ill|lt=myths of Ōkuninushi|Q11433656|qid=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}. Tsutsumi Shrine, which became part of the combined hall, was originally a shrine at Chisha-ga-ike in Hitsu-chō north of this shrine''Unyōshi'' records “Chisha Myōjin enshrines Omoikane-no-Mikoto” (Seki (2006), p.446).. === History === “Hokki-sha,” a shrine with an official of the Jingikan listed in the Shimane District section of the ''Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki,'' is identified with “Hokki Shrine” in Shimane District of Izumo Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. A munafuda from {{ill|lt=Keian|Q1146135|qid=Q1146135|1=Keian}} 1 (1648) records the name “Ōmori Daimyōjin”Seki (2006), p.367. The 1717 gazetteer ''Unyōshi'' records it as “Ōmori Myōjin”. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), in March, it was ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The former shrine site was northeast of the current site, behind the Uguisudai housing area''Unyōshi'' writes this place as “Uguisudani,” and the 1875 ''Kōkoku Chishi'' records it as “Uguisudani” (Seki (2006), pp.367-369).. Beside it is a {{ill|lt=square kofun|Q11504353|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} said to be the “traditional tomb of Umukaihihime-no-Mikoto,” now maintained as a park. === Notes === ===References=== * Kazuhiko Seki, ''Commentary on the Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki'', 2006, {{ill|lt=Akashi Shoten|Q11512528|qid=Q11512528|1=Akashi Shoten}} ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * Yoshiyuki Takine, ''Dictionary of Ancient Izumo'', 2001, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|qid=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}} ISBN 4-404-02941-1 {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hokki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hokki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hokki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]]
